title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: eAnalytics R Shiny web application energy industry analytics, built FERC (Federal Energy Regulatory Commission) data energyr package. main entry point single exported function eAnalytics() launches Shiny app. Supported industry segments: Electric, Natural Gas (companies, pipelines, storage), Hydropower, Oil, LNG.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Linting: Use Air apply tidyverse style. restyle code directly related change.
code: # Load package for interactive development devtools::load_all()  # Run the app eAnalytics::eAnalytics()  # Full R CMD check (equivalent to CI) devtools::check()  # Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps()  # Regenerate documentation from roxygen2 comments roxygen2::roxygenise()  # Run tests devtools::test()

title: CLAUDE.md
text: app monolithic Shiny dashboard (modularized). UI ui.r reactive server logic server.r. Data sourced entirely energyr package startup. industry segment five tabs: Profile (leaflet map), Performance (KPIs), Trends (plotly time-series + DT table), Data (searchable DT table CSV/PDF/Excel export), Explorer (googleVis motion chart).
code: R/eAnalytics.R          # Single exported function — launches the Shiny app inst/app/   global.r              # Loads plotly and shiny at startup   dependencies.r        # Loads all other required packages   ui.r                  # Full dashboard UI (~580 lines)   server.r              # Full server logic (~665 lines)   plotlyGraphWidget.r   # Custom Plotly widget   www/                  # Static web assets (favicon, JS)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Docs roxygen2 comments .R files — never edit .Rd files directly. pkgdown site auto-deployed GitHub Pages push via .github/workflows/pkgdown.yaml. User-facing changes include NEWS.md entry top header.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Three GitHub Actions workflows run push/PR master: - R-CMD-check.yaml — matrix check across macOS, Windows, Ubuntu (release/devel/oldrel-1) - pkgdown.yaml — builds deploys documentation site - pkgcheck.yaml — ropensci automated package checks

text: outlines propose change eAnalytics. detailed discussion contributing tidyverse packages, please see development contributing guide code review principles.

title: Contributing to eAnalytics
text: can fix typos, spelling mistakes, grammatical errors documentation directly using GitHub web interface, long changes made source file. generally means ’ll need edit roxygen2 comments .R, .Rd file. can find .R file generates .Rd reading comment first line.

title: Contributing to eAnalytics
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed). See guide create great issue advice.

title: Contributing to eAnalytics
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("paulgovan/eAnalytics", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to eAnalytics
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use Air apply style, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Contributing to eAnalytics
text: Please note eAnalytics project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project agree abide terms.

title: Getting Started
text: Launching app first brings Home tab, basically landing page gives brief introduction app includes three value boxes current number projects, companies, facilities database.

title: Getting Started
text: Profile tab contains number interactive maps information facilities selected industry. figure shows Profile tab Natural Gas Industry. Multiple options currently available customizing maps. Choose preferred size color variable movable well panel, select different basemaps via lower-right control, click specific facility view additional information.

title: Getting Started
text: Performance tab tracks number Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) selected industry. following figure shows Performance tab Natural Gas Industry.

title: Getting Started
text: Trends tab contains multiple interactive time-series charts financial information selected industry. following figure shows Performance tab Electric industry. time-series chart Trends tab linked data table shown Data tab. Searching, filtering, sorting data data table automatically update time-series chart selected data.

title: Getting Started
text: Data tab contains interactive datatables information selected industry. data can searched, filtered, sorted required. selected data can copied clipboard, downloaded csv pdf file, sent local printer. figure shows Data tab Hydropower industry.

title: Getting Started
text: Explorer tab contains dynamic motion chart exploring several indicators time. following figure shows Explorer tab Natural Gas Industry.

title: Dynamic Web-Based Analytics for the Energy Industry
text: install eAnalytics R: install latest development version: launch app: access app browser, visit paulgovan.shinyapps.io/eAnalytics.

title: Dynamic Web-Based Analytics for the Energy Industry
text: eAnalytics built energyr R package data published United States Federal Energy Regulatory Commission www.ferc.gov. energyr contains several datasets different industry segments: electric: Electric Company Financial Data gas: Natural Gas Company Financial Data hydropower: Hydropower Plant Data lng: LNG Plant Data oil: Oil Company Financial Data pipeline: Natural Gas Pipeline Project Data storage: Natural Gas Storage Field Data
